title: Tie Meshtastic BLE queue-depth tests to Kconfig defaults
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-t2hn.6.2 adds adapter tests mirroring gateway queue bounds with local constants (minimum 17, default 20). Future Kconfig changes could drift from those test constants. What needs to be done: add Kconfig-backed test or build-time assertion coverage so CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_BLE_QUEUE_DEPTH minimum/default assumptions stay aligned with the adapter full-sync boundary tests. Acceptance criteria: a change to the gateway FromRadio queue default/minimum that breaks heartbeat-plus-full-sync headroom fails native_sim/Twister or a compile-time assertion; existing meshtastic_adapter/meshtastic_ble tests still pass.
notes: Implemented Kconfig-backed Meshtastic BLE queue-depth coverage without hardware. Changes: meshtastic_ble test now sources gateway Kconfig via lichen/tests/meshtastic_ble/Kconfig; prj.conf enables CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_BLE=y; removed CMake target_compile_definitions that faked CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_BLE and queue depths; added BUILD_ASSERTs tying CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_BLE_QUEUE_DEPTH_MIN and CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_BLE_QUEUE_DEPTH to 1 + LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_FULL_SYNC_RECORDS(CONFIG_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_NODEDB_MAX_PEERS); added ztest asserting ble_meshtastic_from_radio_capacity() equals the resolved Kconfig default and has full-sync headroom. Explorers: Sartre mapped production Kconfig/use and confirmed current math is heartbeat 1 + full sync 19 = 20, matching current min/default; Newton recommended BUILD_ASSERT coverage for both min and effective default. Reviews: Laplace and Beauvoir reviewed the scoped diff; no P0/P1/P2 findings. Local validation: git diff --check => passed. EC2 validation: launched i-040b59821284ec9ff in us-east-2a with SG sg-02e62b947d1945b66/key lichen-t2hn623-20260702084155; attached EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 at /mnt/lichen-zephyr; ran bootstrap-host.sh and env.sh. Primary EBS worktree was preserved; clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-t2hn623-validate at origin/main f98d224 plus patch. First EC2 Twister attempt failed because local patch omitted new untracked Kconfig file; fixed by adding intent-to-add locally and rerunning with corrected patch including Kconfig. Final EC2 command: west twister -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_ble -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_adapter -p native_sim --inline-logs --outdir twister-out-meshtastic-queue-depth --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-t2hn623-validate/lichen => passed, 2/2 configs, 88 test cases, 0 failed, 0 errored, 0 skipped, 0 warnings, 39.88s. Size: meshtastic_ble native_sim text=119697 data=1704 bss=30610; meshtastic_adapter native_sim text=122975 data=1673 bss=40510. EC2 cleanup complete: sync, unmounted /mnt/lichen-zephyr, detached EBS and confirmed volume available with no attachments, terminated instance, deleted SG and key, removed local temp key/patch/known_hosts.
close_reason: Completed: meshtastic_ble now uses real gateway Kconfig for Meshtastic BLE queue defaults and compile-time asserts both minimum and effective queue depth have heartbeat-plus-full-sync headroom. meshtastic_ble and meshtastic_adapter native_sim Twister passed on EC2; reviews and cleanup evidence recorded in notes.
